enrichment.summary: Kyle records a vlog from his home setup in Boulder, discussing his return to riding a stationary/desk bike, a recent outdoor ride where his Garmin GPS didn't register properly, sleeping deeply after physical exertion, and his theory that brain work burns energy similarly to physical activity. He announces his plan to do daily vlogs documenting 'office bike life' — working at a desk while cycling.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: committed to riding the bike while working every day going forward, no more stopping
expects to need significantly more sleep (up to 12 hours) now that he's riding regularly
can get by on 4-5 hours when sedentary because his brain keeps him going
believes concentrated mental work burns meaningful energy and keeps the body somewhat in shape
touring produces the best sleep of his life — no insomnia, falls asleep immediately from exhaustion
acknowledges it makes him scattered and loses his train of thought; should stop

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: The other video camera suddenly decided that the memory card was incompatible with it.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm gonna go on and get lights tomorrow and put them on this top there so the more light shines on my face.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: This is day two of my daily vlog life.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm gonna do it on the bike from now on and I'm not gonna stop this time.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: The physical pain you feel when you're not on the bike is too big compared to when you're on it.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm gonna start feeling some pain in my back between my clavicle and my spine on the right side from standing up constantly.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'll use my little massage thing to relieve the pain.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Yesterday I got on the bike and rode, but the GPS didn't register the work because I forgot to turn it off.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I had to reattach the sensor and put a new cable in my shifter.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Last night I was really tired and slept really well.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I don't know how much I rode because it didn't work, but it was like a couple of hours.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: As soon as I got in bed, I felt so good.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: One of the funnest things about bike touring is you sleep so well.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: When bike touring, you don't experience much of the discomfort because you're asleep as soon as you lay down.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I could feel my heart pumping while laying in bed, which was an interesting feeling.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I slept until noon today because I needed the sleep.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: My general rule of thumb is like eight to nine hours of sleep.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: The brain uses energy, like a computer, and thinking tires you out.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Eating food or candy can help you think better because it gives you more energy.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I need to start doing videos not high because I was a wreck last time.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I was out of it at work yesterday and forgot stuff I'd never usually forget.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: If I'm sitting around watching YouTube all day, I can get by on four or five hours of sleep.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Sometimes I stay up for 24 hours straight and feel fine.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I've had multiple days in a row where I only got four or five hours of sleep.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm not in bad shape because my brain keeps my body in shape by using energy.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm gonna need more sleep now, like 12 hours.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I can multitask and get almost as much done while riding my bike.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm finally doing this and it's cool to work hard.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm gonna upload these to Strava every day.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: This is my first official ride because stuff wasn't set up yesterday.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'll do a vlog every day talking about the office bike life.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I want to find out how many miles I can do while working in the office.
